mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 2.5% annually, it erodes real corporate margins and triggers defensive portfolio rebalancing, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal a tightening labor market that boosts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative returns in >55% of cases or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, decelerating real GDP growth combined with rising real short-term rates signals demand exhaustion, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to positive during late-cycle phases or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production index values reflect stronger manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports near-term corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO levels.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.2 million units annually, it indicates a broad slowdown in consumer demand and construction activity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising federal funds rates and accelerating inflation creates a stagflationary financial environment that compresses equity multiples, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term fails to improve predictive power over additive models or produces positive returns in >55% of joint-signal instances.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle recoveries, a declining unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions and rising consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when filtered to post-shock recovery regimes or direction reverses to negative.
